name: Kansas v. Garcia
summary: Held that federal immigration law did not preempt Kansas from prosecuting identity fraud, emphasizing the limits of implied preemption under the Supremacy Clause.
significance: Petitioner invoked Justice Alito's opinion for the principle that the Supremacy Clause limits preemption to the Constitution, federal statutes, and treaties — not uncodified federal interests — challenging the theoretical basis of uniquely federal interest preemption.
